setup: Copy the fixture vault to a scratch dir outside the repo, `git init -b main` it, commit everything, and copy skills/next-card and skills/ship-card into <scratch>/.agents/skills/. Run the eval agent with cwd=<scratch>, no network, and `gh` unavailable. See skills/evals/README.md.

evals.name: recommend-and-pause
evals.prompt: What should I pick up next?
evals.expected_output: Runs the ranking script, recommends JB-102 (priority Now, ready — its only dependency JB-101 is Shipped), shows a shortlist including JB-104, notes JB-103 is blocked, and pauses for confirmation. Suggests only — no branch, no file edits.

assertions.text: Recommends JB-102 by id with a one-line rationale
Offers a shortlist or alternatives (JB-104 at minimum)
Uses the fixture's own vocabulary (epic/job, Queued/Doing/Shipped, Now/Soon/Later) — no F-/T- ids, P0 priorities, or other foreign vocabulary
Creates no branch and edits no files; ends awaiting the user's confirmation

evals.name: suggest-only-handoff
evals.prompt: Grab the next card off the board and start working it.
evals.expected_output: Identifies JB-102 as the pick and routes execution through the installed ship-card skill (the user already confirmed by asking to start) instead of building ad hoc. Does not skip ship-card's pre-flight/plan discipline.

assertions.text: Hands execution to the ship-card skill (reads its SKILL.md / follows its flow) rather than improvising the build
Any work done follows ship-card's flow: pre-flight script, plan, branch named job/JB-102-…, frontmatter-only card edits

evals.name: steer-smaller-scope
evals.prompt: I want to knock out the whole field guide epic next — what do you think?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes EP-100 is a container with one unfinished child (JB-102; JB-101 already Shipped). Steers toward the ready child as the smaller first bite but does not refuse the bigger scope — shipping EP-100 whole is the user's call, via ship-card.

assertions.text: Identifies EP-100 as a container and JB-102 as its only unfinished child
Does not refuse the epic outright — acknowledges shipping EP-100 via ship-card is a valid user choice

evals.name: not-a-vault-refusal
evals.prompt: What should I work on next?
evals.expected_output: Run from a directory with no .mos/config.json at or above it: states this isn't a mos vault and stops. No recommendation is invented.

evals.name: parallel-batch
evals.prompt: What can I pick up in parallel right now — a couple of cards I can work at the same time?
evals.expected_output: Runs the script in batch mode (--parallel). Returns a conflict-free batch — JB-102 (prose) and EP-106 (data), whose touches are disjoint — and explains that JB-104 is excluded because its surface collides (prose with JB-102, data with EP-106). Names the overlapping area, not just the exclusion. Suggests only; creates no branch and edits nothing.

assertions.text: Returns JB-102 and EP-106 as a parallel batch (their touches are disjoint)
Excludes JB-104 and names the colliding area(s) — prose with JB-102 and/or data with EP-106 — not a bare exclusion
Uses the fixture's own vocabulary (epic/job, area names prose/layout/data) — no F-/T- ids or other foreign vocabulary
Creates no branch and edits no files; ends awaiting confirmation

evals.name: parallel-batch-no-areas
evals.prompt: What can I pick up in parallel right now?
evals.expected_output: Special setup: the top-level `areas` key has been removed from .mos/config.json, so the vault declares no surfaces. Batch mode degrades to the plain ready set (JB-102, EP-106, JB-104) with an explicit caveat that file overlap can't be checked — it does NOT claim the cards are proven safe to parallelize.

assertions.text: Returns the ready set rather than refusing
States plainly that overlap is unknown / unchecked because the vault declares no areas — does not assert the cards are collision-free
Creates no branch and edits no files
